reasoning: The FRED statistical tool combined estimate suggests around a 51% chance of currently bouncing above 5.71, but momentum is strongly downward (4 consecutive weeks). Daily tracking via Zillow and other sources around August 15-17 show the 15-year fixed rate ticking up slightly to 5.70%, but this is still technically below the 5.71% threshold from August 14. This implies the next weekly reading (for Aug 21) risks coming in flat or slightly below. Looking further out, consensus forecasts and macroeconomic signals (including slight inflation softness and pricing in of potential Fed cuts later in 2025) lean toward a gentle decline in mortgage rates through the end of the year. Thus, the probability of exceeding the current 5.71% benchmark is slightly less than 50% across the board. Per the forecasting heuristics, uncertainty increases with horizon, leading to the probabilities gradually shifting closer to a baseline 0.50 for the furthest resolution date in February 2026.
